user_story: As a family caregiver, I want to ask Qwen for tomorrow clinic preparation in my own words.
expected_behavior: A bottom/central chat composer accepts an editable question and sends it without hidden fixed text.
code_evidence: App.tsx now renders PromptComposer with editable textarea and Send submit.
ux_error: Resolved: user can see and edit the caregiver prompt.
fix_direction: Keep prompt visible as the first interaction surface.

user_story: As a judge, I want to compare No Memory, Raw Memory, and ERINYS + Qwen from the same user question.
expected_behavior: After one send, three answer panels appear side by side with clear mode labels and risk/value framing.
code_evidence: App.tsx now renders AnswerCard columns for the three modes plus Answer Diff banner.
ux_error: Resolved: same-prompt comparison is now the main layout.
fix_direction: Keep the three actual answer bodies as the primary visual center.

user_story: As a judge, I want to see the generic answer quality without memory.
expected_behavior: The baseline answer should look useful but shallow, showing what Qwen misses without context.
code_evidence: No Memory card now shows baseline badge and missing-context outcomes.
ux_error: Resolved: missing taxi / medication notebook / personal context is explicit.
fix_direction: Keep baseline visibly useful but shallow.

user_story: As a judge, I want to see why passing all memory directly is dangerous.
expected_behavior: Raw Memory answer visibly includes stale routines or private identifiers and receives a risk warning.
code_evidence: qwen_adapter mock now exposes synthetic private ID/address and stale train/ramen routine for the Raw Memory mode.
ux_error: Resolved: dangerous snippets are highlighted in the Raw Memory answer.
fix_direction: Keep all risky data synthetic and clearly marked unsafe.

user_story: As a caregiver, I want the final answer to use only current safe care context.
expected_behavior: ERINYS + Qwen answer should mention afternoon appointment, taxi, medication notebook, low-sodium food, and avoid private identifiers.
code_evidence: ERINYS + Qwen card now has governed badge and green highlighted safe snippets.
ux_error: Resolved: governed answer is positioned as the best answer without making it a marketing hero.
fix_direction: Keep safe care briefing concise and non-diagnostic.

user_story: As a judge, I want to know why each memory was selected, demoted, contradicted, or blocked.
expected_behavior: Right-side audit console displays counts and an inspectable decision list with reasons.
code_evidence: App.tsx now renders MemoryDecisionPanel as persistent right rail.
ux_error: Resolved: audit is no longer buried inside one answer card.
fix_direction: Keep decision panel secondary to the three answer bodies.

user_story: As a caregiver, I want private identifiers never exposed in the governed answer.
expected_behavior: Sensitive records are blocked and shown as blocked in audit, not included in Qwen context.
code_evidence: Decision panel lists blocked synthetic insurance/address records and governed answer avoids them.
ux_error: Resolved: privacy win is visible in Raw versus ERINYS comparison.
fix_direction: Add redacted preview later only if it does not steal focus from answer diff.

user_story: As a caregiver, I want old routines ignored when newer care constraints conflict.
expected_behavior: Stale routines are demoted or contradicted, and the final answer follows newer memory.
code_evidence: TransformColumn and MemoryDecisionPanel show demotion of stale routine while ERINYS answer uses taxi/avoid stairs.
ux_error: Resolved: stale routine loses to current care context on screen.

user_story: As a judge, I want to see that governance reduces context size.
expected_behavior: UI shows raw memory token estimate versus governed prompt token estimate and percent saved.
code_evidence: TokenEfficiency strip now compares Raw Memory and ERINYS context tokens.
ux_error: Remaining issue: current seed only shows 29% savings
fix_direction: which is less dramatic than the target mockup.
retest_status: Adjust seed size or selected-memory threshold if stronger token-savings demo is needed.

user_story: As a hackathon judge, I want to understand the product in 10 seconds during a video.
expected_behavior: The first viewport says: same question, three answers, ERINYS chooses safe memory for Qwen.
code_evidence: Top banner now says Same prompt three memory strategies and the first viewport shows the answer diff.
ux_error: Resolved: first viewport reveals the interaction model.
fix_direction: Use this screen as the video opening shot.
